TORNADO TRAVELED 4.3 MILES AND CAUSED EF1 DAMAGE TO ROOFS, WINDOWS, AND GARAGE DOORS ON HOMES NEAR TOBEN AND SOUTH STONEY CREEK RDS IN FRENCHTOWN TOWNSHIP IN ADDITION TO MANY TREES THAT WERE DAMAGED OR UPROOTED. MAXIMUM WINDS AROUND 95-100 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 520 YARDS.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. 3 HOMES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE NEAR TOBEN RD AND SOUTH STONEY CREEK RD. HOMES ARE UNINHABITABLE.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. MONROE COUNTY CENTRAL DISPATCH REPORTED TREES AND WIRES DOWN IN FRENCHTOWN TOWNSHIP.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE BLOCKING ROADS NEAR LABO RD IN BERLIN TOWNSHIP.

TORNADO TRAVELED 4 MILES FROM BERLIN TOWNSHIP IN MONROE COUNTY TO WAYNE COUNTY NEAR THE BORDER OF BROWNSTOWN TOWNSHIP AND THE CITY OF GIBRALTAR. EF0 DAMAGE WAS EXHIBITED WITH MULTIPLE LIMBS AND LARGE TREES DOWNED ALONG WITH MISSING SHINGLES TO A HOME NEAR THE END OF ITS PATH. MAXIMUM WINDS AROUND 60-65 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.

TORNADO TRAVELED 3.3 MILES FROM W JEFFERSON AVE / GIBRALTAR RD IN GIBRALTAR ACROSS THE DETROIT RIVER TO THE NORTH END OF ELBA ISLAND IN GROSSE ILE TOWNSHIP. EF1 DAMAGE SEEN AT THE BEGINNING OF ITS PATH, INCLUDING MULTIPLE LARGE LIMBS, TREES, AND POWERLINES DOWNED AND A ROOF PARTIALLY BLOWN OFF. MAXIMUM WINDS AROUND 95-100 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN GIBRALTER.
